notes: Iteration 10 FULL MODE: Addresses score regression 7.0->6.5 from iter-9 review. THREE priorities: (1) Phase 0 zero-GPU paper integrity fixes (BLOCKING), (2) Phase 1 probe degradation ablation (H10 -- HIGHEST PRIORITY new experiment), (3) Phase 2 remaining experiments (H8 first-letter decoder magnitude, H9 rate-distortion with more pairs). Builds on corrected FULL-mode data: activation patching city-continent d=1.50, city-language d=0.75 (bugfix from d=-0.91 pilot). Total: ~3.5 GPU-hours, ~10 wall-clock hours with 1 GPU.

tasks.name: Phase 0.1: Data Integrity Overhaul + validate_integration.py
tasks.description: Create data provenance infrastructure. (1) Build exp/results/data_manifest.json mapping every numerical claim in the paper to its source JSON file + field path. Cover: absorption rates (H1), patching effect sizes (H7), hedging percentages (H3), probe F1s, architecture comparison, benign/pathological logit changes. (2) Resolve known discrepancies: F1=0.97 vs 1.0 for first-letter probes (sae_spelling binary probe vs sklearn logistic regression); benign count 1471 vs 1464; aggregation method (per-unique-word vs per-token). (3) Implement exp/code/validate_integration.py: reads data_manifest.json + paper LaTeX, extracts claims, cross-checks against source JSON, reports mismatches. Exit 0 if all match. (4) Run validation and fix any discovered mismatches. Source data: iter_009/exp/results/full/consolidation_summary.json, iter_009/exp/results/full/activation_patching_crossdomain_full.json, iter_009/exp/results/phase1/*.json, iter_009/exp/results/phase2/*.json.

pilot.pass_criteria: data_manifest.json created with >= 20 claim mappings. validate_integration.py runs successfully. All known discrepancies documented with resolution.

tasks.name: Phase 0.2-0.4: Propagate Corrected Data + Reframe Claims
tasks.description: Three blocking paper corrections combined into one task. (A) Step 0.2: Update Section 5.2 with corrected FULL-mode cross-domain patching: city-continent d=1.50 (from iter_009/exp/results/full/activation_patching_crossdomain_full.json), city-language d=0.75. Remove concentrated vs. distributed absorption dichotomy. Update abstract with universal mechanism claim. Verify: grep for 'd=-0.91' and '0.05% recovery' returns 0 hits. (B) Step 0.3: Reframe benign/pathological in Section 5.3. Acknowledge circularity: diagnostic measures decoder geometry, not computational redundancy. Reframe from '100% pathological' to 'child decoders carry large-magnitude parent information (3.98 nats vs 0.12 control)'. Describe genuine test requirements. Scope to city-continent L24 only. (C) Step 0.4: Reframe headline claims. '4x range' qualified as descriptive. '100% pathological' scoped. 'No architecture effect' reframed as underpowered. Kruskal-Wallis scope clarified (3 RAVEL hierarchies). Separate descriptive from inferential claims.

pilot.pass_criteria: Paper sections updated. grep for 'd=-0.91' returns 0. Circularity acknowledged in Section 5.3. Headline claims scoped with qualifiers. Correction log JSON documenting all changes.

tasks.name: Phase 0.5: Generate Missing Figures 4-6
tasks.description: Generate three missing PDF figures from existing JSON data. (1) fig4_patching_comparison.pdf: paired dot plot showing recovery rates by hierarchy type (first-letter d=1.33, city-continent d=1.50, city-language d=0.75). Each pair = child-zeroed vs control recovery. Source: iter_009/exp/results/full/activation_patching_crossdomain_full.json + iter_008 first-letter patching. (2) fig5_pathological_histogram.pdf: histogram of |logit change| values from decoder direction ablation with random-direction control overlay. Source: iter_009/exp/results/phase2/benign_pathological.json. (3) fig6_architecture_comparison.pdf: grouped bar chart of absorption rates by architecture (JumpReLU, BatchTopK, Matryoshka) x hierarchy (first-letter, city-continent, city-country, city-language). Source: iter_009/exp/results/phase1/architecture_comparison.json or iter_008/exp/results/phase4/architecture_comparison.json. Use matplotlib with publication-quality settings (300 DPI, tight layout, no gridlines, seaborn palette).

pilot.pass_criteria: All 3 PDF figures generated. Each figure renders correctly with axes labels, legend, and title. File sizes > 10KB (not empty).

visualizations.description: Activation patching recovery: child-zeroed vs control by hierarchy

tasks.name: Environment Verification for GPU Tasks
tasks.description: Quick sanity check that environment is functional for GPU experiments. Verify: (1) SAELens >=6.39 importable, (2) TransformerLens importable, (3) GPU available with >=24GB VRAM, (4) Gemma 2 2B model loadable (test tokenizer only for speed), (5) Gemma Scope SAE L24-16k loadable, (6) RAVEL dataset accessible (hij/ravel), (7) sae_spelling importable, (8) scipy, sklearn, numpy, matplotlib importable. (9) Verify iter_009 probe files exist: iter_009/exp/results/phase1/sae_spelling_probes/layer_24/. All confirmed working in iter_009 -- this is fast verification. Output: setup_check.json with versions and GPU info.

pilot.pass_criteria: All packages importable. GPU detected with >=24GB VRAM. Iter_009 probe files found.

tasks.name: Phase 1.1: Probe Degradation Ablation (H10 -- HIGHEST PRIORITY)
tasks.description: THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT EXPERIMENT. Resolves whether cross-domain variation is genuine or a probe artifact. Method: (1) Load trained first-letter probes at L24 from iter_009/exp/results/phase1/sae_spelling_probes/layer_24/. These have F1=1.0 (sae_spelling binary probes). (2) For each target F1 level in {0.70, 0.80, 0.85, 0.90}: inject label noise into the TEST SET to degrade probe test-set F1 to the target. Use seed=42. Strategy: randomly flip labels for N randomly selected test examples until measured F1 drops to target (binary search on N). Verify degradation by re-evaluating probe F1 on corrupted test set. (3) For each degradation level: re-run the full absorption measurement pipeline at L24 with JumpReLU 16k SAE. Use the SAME absorption measurement code as iter_009 phase1_absorption_firstletter but with the degraded probes. Measure: absorption_rate, n_false_negatives, n_absorbed, bootstrap 95% CI (10k resamples). (4) Also run at ORIGINAL F1=1.0 as internal control -- must match iter_009 results within CI (27.1% +/- CI). (5) Create absorption_rate vs probe_F1 plot with RAVEL hierarchy points overlaid at their F1 levels: city-continent (42.9% at F1=0.84), city-country (45.1% at F1=0.79), city-language (11.6% at F1=0.82). (6) Statistical analysis: linear regression of absorption_rate ~ probe_F1, extrapolation to RAVEL F1 levels, comparison with actual RAVEL rates. (7) Key test: at F1=0.80, does first-letter absorption rate fall within 5 pp of RAVEL rates? If yes: variation is probe-driven. If no: hierarchy effect is genuine. BOTH outcomes publishable. Report in new Section 4.6 or appendix.
tasks.comment_on_duration: 4 degradation levels + 1 control x ~10 min each for absorption measurement at L24 16k. Conservative estimate includes model + SAE loading time.

pilot.pass_criteria: Absorption rates computed at all 5 F1 levels (0.70, 0.80, 0.85, 0.90, 1.0). F1=1.0 control within CI of iter_009 result (27.1%). Clear monotonic trend in absorption_rate vs probe_F1. Comparison with RAVEL rates documented.

visualizations.description: Absorption rate vs probe F1 for first-letter, with RAVEL hierarchy points overlaid

tasks.name: Phase 2.1: Decoder Direction Magnitude on First-Letter (H8 Cross-Domain Consistency)
tasks.description: Replicate the decoder direction ablation diagnostic on FIRST-LETTER at L24 16k, extending the city-continent-only result (3.98 nats, 0% benign). Method: (1) Load Gemma 2B + Gemma Scope L24 16k SAE. (2) Identify all first-letter absorption instances from iter_009 data (or re-identify using probes). (3) For each absorbed token context: (a) Get SAE reconstruction. (b) Ablate parent direction component from child latent's decoder vector: d_child_modified = d_child - (d_child . d_parent / ||d_parent||^2) * d_parent. (c) Run model with modified SAE decoder. (d) Measure logit change for correct letter tokens. (4) Classify: benign (|logit_change| <= 0.1), pathological (|logit_change| > 0.1). Also test at 0.05 and 0.2. (5) Control: ablate random direction of same norm. (6) Compare distribution with city-continent result (3.98 nats mean). (7) Report: is pathological severity consistent across hierarchy types? Does first-letter (F1=1.0, concentrated mechanism) show different magnitude than city-continent (F1=0.84, distributed)? This addresses the reframing: the diagnostic measures decoder information magnitude, not computational redundancy.

pilot.pass_criteria: Logit changes computed for first-letter absorption instances. Distribution comparable or different from city-continent (3.98 nats). Random direction control shows near-zero logit change. Classification at 3 thresholds (0.05, 0.1, 0.2).

visualizations.description: Decoder direction magnitude: first-letter vs city-continent comparison

tasks.name: Phase 2.2: Rate-Distortion Predictor Validation (Pooled Cross-Domain, H9)
tasks.description: Re-test the three-factor predictor model with more parent-child pairs pooled across all hierarchies and corrected data. Iter-9 tested 20 pairs, found rho=0.261 (p=0.266). Method: (1) Pool ALL identified parent-child pairs from iter_009 experiments: first-letter absorption pairs + city-continent pairs + city-language pairs. Target: >=40 pairs (up from 20). (2) For each pair, compute: cos_sim = cosine(d_parent, d_child) from SAE decoder vectors at L24 16k, co_occur = P(child active | parent active) from activation statistics over 10k+ sequences, R_parent = MSE increase when parent direction ablated from SAE decoder (or proxy: mean parent activation magnitude). (3) Fit linear model: absorption_prob ~ beta_1*cos_sim^2 + beta_2*co_occur - beta_3*R_parent. (4) Evaluate: Spearman rho between predicted and observed. Also test each predictor individually. (5) Cross-domain analysis: do cos_sim / co_occur distributions differ between hierarchies? Does this explain absorption rate differences? (6) If rho < 0.3 or p > 0.05: report as definitive negative result alongside GAS and CMI. The cross-domain characterization stands independently of predictive models. Source pairs: iter_009/exp/results/phase2/activation_patching_crossdomain.json (cross-domain pairs), iter_009/exp/results/phase1/absorption_firstletter.json (first-letter pairs).

pilot.pass_criteria: Per-pair predictors computed for >= 30 pairs across hierarchies. Spearman rho computed with p-value. Individual predictor correlations reported. Cross-domain predictor distribution analysis.

visualizations.description: Three-factor model: predicted vs observed absorption (pooled cross-domain)
Individual and combined predictor correlations with absorption

tasks.name: Phase 3.1: Write Appendix Sections (Negative Results + Threshold Sensitivity)
tasks.description: CPU-only compilation of appendix content from existing data. (1) GAS negative result appendix: rho=0.116, AUROC=0.571. Explain failure: GAS captures decoder geometry but not encoder competitive exclusion. Source: iter_008/exp/results/phase2/gas_full.json or iter_009/exp/results/phase4/negative_results.json. (2) CMI negative result: rho=0.044, p=0.83 at L0=22. Explain: information-theoretic approach does not capture absorption. (3) Absorption Tax appendix: T(G)=0.414, ranking rho=-0.20, concordance 50%. Retained as qualitative framework. (4) Threshold sensitivity: CV=0.077, 20/20 cells robust. Source: iter_001/exp/results/full/ablation_threshold_sensitivity.json. (5) Rate-distortion predictor (from Phase 2.2, or iter_009 if Phase 2.2 confirms negative). Output: structured JSON summaries per appendix section, suitable for writing agent.

pilot.pass_criteria: All 5 appendix section summaries generated with specific metrics, failure analysis, and paper section assignments.

tasks.name: Phase 3.2: Document Methodological Details
tasks.description: CPU-only documentation task. (1) Document per-unique-word aggregation method used in absorption measurement: each word contributes one absorption rate (average over contexts), not one per token occurrence. Explain why: prevents high-frequency words from dominating statistics. (2) Document token position asymmetry: first-letter uses position -6 (sae_spelling convention, word positioned 6 tokens from end), RAVEL uses position -2 (entity at second-to-last position). Explain why different and assess impact. (3) Document single-model limitation: Gemma 2 2B only. No cross-model validation. Future work would test on GPT-2, Pythia, Llama. (4) Document probe quality confound: F1 correlation with absorption (rho=-0.756). This motivates H10. (5) Add reproducibility section: list all seeds, library versions, SAE IDs. Output: methodology_supplements.json suitable for writing agent.

pilot.pass_criteria: All 5 methodological notes documented with clear explanations and paper section assignments.

tasks.name: Phase 4: Final Consolidation and Writing Gate
tasks.description: Aggregate ALL iter_010 experimental results into comprehensive summary. (1) Compile probe degradation ablation results (H10 verdict: genuine hierarchy effect OR probe artifact). (2) Compile decoder direction magnitude on first-letter (H8 cross-domain consistency). (3) Compile rate-distortion pooled results (H9 verdict: confirmed NOT_SUPPORTED or upgraded). (4) Update ALL hypothesis verdicts: H1-H10. Include H10 for the first time. (5) Cross-reference with iter_009 consolidation for consistency. (6) Writing gate: go_write=true if: (a) H10 provides clear answer (either direction), (b) corrected FULL-mode data propagated to paper, (c) all figures generated. (7) Generate per-paper-section result summaries for writing agent. (8) List remaining open issues for next iteration (if any). (9) Compile data_manifest verification results from Phase 0.

pilot.pass_criteria: consolidation_summary.json with all 10 hypothesis verdicts (H1-H10), writing gate decision, per-section result summaries, data integrity verification, and open issues list.

parallelization_note: Wave 1: data integrity + figures + env check + methodology docs (1 GPU + 3 CPU tasks, 45 min). Wave 2: paper corrections + probe degradation + decoder magnitude + rate-distortion (1 GPU for experiments, CPU for corrections; 55 min serial or 55 min with 3 GPUs parallel). Wave 3: appendix writing (CPU, 30 min). Wave 4: consolidation (CPU, 25 min). Total wall-clock: ~2.5 hours with 3 GPUs, ~4 hours with 1 GPU.

fallback_decisions.if_probe_degradation_shows_artifact: Rewrite cross-domain finding as probe-quality-dependent. Add probe-quality-matched comparison as the corrected metric. This is an important methodological contribution regardless.
fallback_decisions.if_probe_degradation_noise_injection_fai: Try alternative degradation strategies: (1) subsample training data to degrade probe, (2) add Gaussian noise to probe weights, (3) use probes trained with fewer epochs. At least one method should achieve target F1 levels.
fallback_decisions.if_decoder_magnitude_differs_on_firstlet: Report as hierarchy-dependent decoder information content. Both consistent and different outcomes are informative about absorption mechanics.
fallback_decisions.if_rate_distortion_still_fails_with_more: Report as definitive 3rd negative result (alongside GAS, CMI). The triple failure strengthens the case that interventional methods (activation patching) are necessary for absorption detection.
fallback_decisions.if_data_integrity_reveals_more_errors: Fix all errors using validate_integration.py as regression test. Document corrections in paper_corrections_log.json. Do NOT proceed to writing gate until all claims verified.
fallback_decisions.if_env_setup_fails: Reinstall: pip install sae-lens transformer-lens. If VRAM insufficient, reduce batch sizes. If Gemma Scope SAE unavailable, try alternative SAE release.

paper_framing.contribution_1: First cross-domain absorption characterization + probe degradation ablation (strengthens or corrects the finding)
paper_framing.contribution_2: Universal causal mechanism via activation patching (corrected FULL-mode d=0.75-1.50). Tightened hedging (strict 0-22.6% vs loose 92.6%).
paper_framing.contribution_3: Decoder direction magnitude analysis with acknowledged circularity. Cross-hierarchy consistency (new from Phase 2.1).
paper_framing.key_new_element_iter10: H10 probe degradation ablation resolves the core ambiguity. Either outcome is a significant contribution.
